title: COORDINATED ENTRY FOR FAMILIES MULTNOMAH COUNTY - 211info
text: 211INFO COORDINATED ENTRY FOR FAMILIES MULTNOMAH COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Multnomah County Coordinated Entry for Families is a partnership of different agencies through Multnomah County’s Homeless Service Department (HSD) and consists of both the Supportive Housing & Family Shelter program. 211info completes eligibility and program enrollment via a Multnomah Services and Screening Tool (MSST) assessment. If families are eligible, partnering agencies within the community connect with families and offer services related to shelter/housing access. Meeting eligibility requirements does not guarantee access to services. Households are prioritized for assistance based on various factors, including availability of resources. This is not an immediate service and the wait time can be a few months to a year. It is encouraged that households continue seeking other resources and support. This program CANNOT assist financially with evictions to maintain current housing. It may be able to help with future housing if the household becomes homeless. Eligibility: Families with children in their custody under the age of 18 (or plans for reunification) - OR - a third trimester pregnancy (28 weeks/7 months) with connections to Multnomah County (i.e., was displaced, has kids in school, job, service providers in Multnomah County, etc.) AND meet one of the following criteria: -In emergency shelter, transitional housing, motel paid for by an agency or a place not meant for habitation (i.e. car, streets, tent, abandoned building, etc.) -Doubled up (with family/friends and not on the lease, unsafely doubled up/living situation is over safe capacity) and at risk of becoming literally homeless within 14 days -Housed, including self pay at a motel, and will become literally homeless within 14 days or has received a court ordered eviction notice OR has a self-identified EHV voucher set to expire in Fall of 2026 -In an institution (i.e. jail, hospital, substance use or mental health treatment facility, etc.) and at risk of exiting to homelessness within 14 days -Fleeing or attempting to flee domestic violence and lacks the resources or support networks to obtain other safe, permanent housing Hours: Added to callback List for Assessment: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Assessment: 7 days per week 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers 211 211info (503) 222-5555 Alternate (503) 226-3099 Administrative Line 898211 Text Short Code Intake 211 Alternate (503) 222-5555 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call 211info to be added to the callback list to be assessed for the program. After being added to the callback list, a Homeless Services Specialist from 211 will call back within 48 hours to complete the Multnomah Services and Screening Tool (MSST) assessment. Family/friends/caseworkers can add individuals needing services to the callback list, however to complete the full assessment, the head of household will be contacted directly within a few days by a Homeless Services Specialist from 211. Family/friends/caseworkers can sit on the call during the assessment to assist the household but cannot complete the assessment alone on behalf of the family. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required to complete assessment. Some may be required once assigned to a caseworker. LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County Services: Homeless Permanent Supportive Housing * Homeless Families Homeless Permanent Supportive Housing * Pregnant Individuals Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Homeless Families Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Pregnant Individuals Other Services Offered At This Location: DISASTER SERVICES - 211INFO DCM LINE DISASTER SERVICES - GORGE CITIZEN ALERT PROGRAM DISASTER SERVICES - SEVERE WINTER WEATHER INFORMATION SERVICES DISASTER SERVICES - WINTER AND SEVERE WEATHER INFORMATION SERVICES MULTNOMAH COUNTY FOOD - 211INFO SNAP AND FOOD ACCESS HOUSING/SHELTER - MULTNOMAH COUNTY EVICTION PREVENTION PROGRAM HOUSING/SHELTER - MULTNOMAH COUNTY FAMILY SHELTER WAITLIST INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - 211INFO CHILD CARE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - 211INFO COORDINATION CENTER INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT INFORMATION SERVICES - 211INFO ICE IMPACT SUPPORT INFORMATION SERVICES - 211INFO INFORMATION AND REFERRAL INFORMATION SERVICES - 211INFO OUTREACH AND MATERIALS REQUESTS INFORMATION SERVICES - 211INFO RESOURCE DEPARTMENT INFORMATION SERVICES - OREGON HIV/STD HOTLINE MONEY MANAGEMENT - 211INFO FINANCIAL WELLNESS UTILITIES - 211INFO UTILITY ASSISTANCE UPDATES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
